title: Vijay Karur visit LIVE: Chief Minister announces memorial for stampede victims on behalf of TVK
content: The president of Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam, during his speech, termed the DMK the "vending machine" of corruption.

title: BJP urges TVK govt. to address vacancies in State-run arts and science colleges
content: It was a bitter truth that students did not prefer State-run colleges that lacked qualified teachers, necessary buildings, modern labs, and basic amenities, Mr. Nagenthran said

title: Vizianagaram to hold special SIR campaign on July 11, 12 ahead of deadline
content: About 15.90 lakh forms have been distributed and 75% uploaded; the district administration will hold a weekend drive before the July 14 deadline, with the draft roll due on July 21

title: Take up GST cut on commercial LPG with Centre, hoteliers ask Andhra government
content: A delegation of the Andhra Pradesh Star Hotels Association met Civil Supplies Minister Nadendla Manohar, seeking a lower GST on commercial LPG cylinders and the restoration of input tax credit for the sector

title: Hooda-Surjewala mic exchange sparks off discussion on power dynamics in Haryana Congress
content: The timing of the exchange between the two leaders too matters. The party’s narrow loss in the 2024 Assembly polls has revived internal demands for change

title: TTD suspends all special darshan formats as it gears up for twin Brahmotsavams
content: All departments have been directed to complete preparations well ahead of schedule to handle the expected surge in pilgrim footfall during the festivities commencing from September 8

title: What led to deaths after C-section deliveries in Kota hospitals | Explained
content: The discovery of the fake oxytocin injections has once again highlighted India’s very critical problem of drug quality and safety.

title: No privatisation of APSRTC, e-buses won’t transfer assets to private firms: government
content: The induction of electric buses under the Centre’s PM e-Bus Sewa Scheme does not involve the transfer of RTC assets or services to private entities, Special Chief Secretary (Transport) Krishna Babu has said

title: US firm CorroHealth closure in Kerala: No breakthrough in Minister-level meeting
content: Kerala govt issues ultimatum to medical coding firm to present alternative proposals to retain its workforce in the State or face prosecution for alleged violations of labour laws

title: Panel to be formed soon to examine liquor retail models in other States: T.N. Minister for Prohibition Vignesh
content: The committee will study the functioning of excise departments in Kerala, Karnataka, Telangana, Andhra Pradesh, and Odisha, including liquor procurement, retail operations, and employee service conditions, says K. Vignesh

title: Football fans in Kerala transform their homes into colourful tributes to their teams
content: For these two fans, who live near Kochi, who have painted their houses in the colours of the countries they support in the FiFA World Cup, football is more than a seasonal obsession

title: Assam govt. tables ₹2,85,084 crore Budget for FY27
content: Presenting his maiden Budget, Assam Finance Minister Jayanta Malla Baruah also announced to continue all the major schemes rolled out during the last five years, while stating that he aims to cut Budget deficit to ₹419 crore

title: Karur stampede: Madurai Bench of Madras HC permits T.N. govt. to conduct event to issue appointment orders
content: The court, however, said the employment offer would be on a temporary basis, subject to judicial review

title: Lokesh proposes Andhra-Korea Investment Desk, invites Kia to expand near Visakhapatnam
content: On the fifth day of his South Korea tour, the Minister invited Kia to set up EV and export facilities near Visakhapatnam Port and sought Korean partners for the State’s data centre and renewable energy plans

title: E20 petrol may cut mileage by up to 5% but offers cleaner combustion, energy security benefits: Ministry
content: The Ministry rejected concerns that the programme had been implemented too quickly, saying India's ethanol blending initiative dates back to pilot projects launched in 2001, with 5% blending introduced in parts of the country by 2006

title: Why is Gujarat betting  ₹6 lakh crore on data centres? | Explained
content: The State hopes to attract investments worth ₹6 lakh crore and build 7.5 GW of data centre capacity over the next few years, positioning itself alongside established hubs such as Maharashtra, Tamil Nadu and Telangana

title: Use hydel generation only to meet peak hour demand, official directs AP-Genco
content: He directed it to maximise thermal power generation by operating all available units at full capacity, strengthening preventive maintenance and minimising outages

title: Kozhikode railway station in Kerala restores train services after clock tower collapse
content: Debris clearance and demolition work continue at the site following the structural failure during monsoon rains

title: Leaders voicing their opinion were unique to Congress: Punjab in-charge Baghel
content: “Unlike in other parties, it is not a one-way communication and command in the Congress. The Congress values and respects the seniority and contribution of each leader, which is acknowledged and rewarded at the right time,” says Bhupesh Baghel

title: Is there any bar on allowing outsiders in Cabinet meetings? | Explained
content: On July 4, del­eag­a­tions of the DMK, the AIADMK and the BJP met Gov­ernor Rajendra Vish­wanath Arlekar and sub­mit­ted sep­ar­ate memor­anda urging him to ini­ti­ate legal action against the par­ti­cip­a­tion of “two private indi­vidu­als” in a recently held Cab­inet meeting

title: GBA seeks Supreme Court nod to postpone Bengaluru civic polls to December, cites Karnataka SIR
content: In its applications filed on July 9, the Greater Bengaluru Authority said the whole administrative machinery and human resources of the five city corporations were fully engaged in the SIR exercise

title: PM Modi leaves for New Zealand after concluding Australia visit
content: During his two-day visit, PM Modi will meet with New Zealand Prime Minister Christopher Luxon and other leaders, focusing on enhancing economic and trade relations

title: 34-metre cycling track stretch removed in Narsingi for U-turn to decongest traffic flow
content: Traffic police expect the new underpass link to reduce congestion by around 30%, but cyclists say repeated removal of dedicated cycling infrastructure sets a worrying precedent

title: Bihar Bankipur bypoll: NDA leaders confident of BJP candidate’s victory
content: Jan Suraaj Party has fielded its founder Prashant Kishor whereas Opposition Rashtriya Janata Dal has fielded Rekha Gupta in bypoll

title: India lays out tariffs and quotas for U.K. vehicles under trade deal
content: As per the Directorate General of Foreign Trade’s notification, a total of 20,000 completely built units (CBUs) of petrol and diesel passenger vehicles will be allowed for import from the U.K. at concessional rates of 30-50%, depending on the size of the car, down from the normal import duty of between 66-110%

title: Hit-and-run twist in fatal accident in Kerala capital; car driver nabbed based on CCTV evidence
content: Police apprehend car driver after establishing that the motorcycle was hit from behind before the four-wheeler sped away from the scene in Thiruvallam. Two young IT professionals were killed in the incident

title: Maharashtra government orders probe into church lands
content: Maharashtra Revenue Minister Chandrashekhar Bawankule informed the Legislative Assembly that a review of all land records linked to Christian missions in the State is underway.

title: Andhra Pradesh aims to bring 60 lakh farmers under natural farming by 2035
content: Twenty-Point Programme Implementation Chairman Lanka Dinakar said chemical fertilizer use had fallen over 10% and the State expected about ₹300 crore under the Centre’s PM PRANAM scheme

title: Sliding rupee value comes as a blessing for Telangana’s Musi project
content: ADB has given approval for USD500 million,which translated into ₹4,100 crore over a year ago when the loan was sought. With rupee value sliding substantially since then, the actual funding has now reached ₹4,500 crore.

title: Calcutta High Court allows Trinamool to operate frozen accounts under judicial watch
content: The Calcutta High Court appointed retired  High Court judge Subrata Talukda as special officer to countersign every cheque; TMC accounts hold ₹440 crore frozen since June on rebel MLA’s complaint

title: Karnataka CM inaugurates Syed Mohammad Gesu Daraz Research Academy at Khwaja Banda Nawaz Dargah
content: The academy has been conceived to preserve, research and disseminate the life, teachings, literary works and rare manuscripts of Hazrat Khwaja Banda Nawaz, one of the most influential Sufi saints of the Deccan

title: Andhra Pradesh police being misused to target critics, alleges YSRCP’s Lakshmi Parvathi
content: The law and order situation in Andhra Pradesh has deteriorated under the coalition government, says  Lakshmi Parvathi

title: ₹200 crore sanctioned for tap water to every home in Machilipatnam: Kollu Ravindra
content: Proposals have been prepared for improving roads in residential colonies and widening approach road to the Machilipatnam Medical College, says Ravindra

title: ‘Kashmir not selling Disneyland; protecting lakes, glaciers, mountains central to tourism plan,’ says CM Omar Abdullah
content: The Chief Minister said he welcomed a surge in the number of tourists visiting J&K, but in the future, the tourist flow may need to be regulated so that not only the tourist resorts are protected, but the tourists also enjoy a serene environment rather than having to go through a harrowing experience

title: NGT orders recovery of ₹2.65 crore fine from BHU for illegally cutting 33 trees
content: On August 11 last year, the NGT had directed the UPPCB to assess and recover environmental compensation within three months, but it was not done

title: SIR: Anomalies and unmapped voters could land 30% forms for scrutiny in Telangana
content: The electors who are unmapped and whose information has anomalies would receive notices from the EROs after the completion of distribution and collection of forms

title: Abolish adjustment charges, roll back smart meters: CPI(M) to Andhra Pradesh govt
content: The government’s decision to reduce adjustment charges by only 13 paise per unit from January 2026 was intended to create a false impression of tariff relief, says Babu Rao

title: SIR: Residents flag purple stickers pasted despite no enumeration forms being distributed
content: Concerns were also reported from Banaswadi, Benson Town, and in apartment complexes in Bellandur and Varthur, where residents said there appeared to be no uniform procedure being followed

title: Koli community demands mandatory registration, auditing of RSS
content: Led by community leader Lacchappa Jamadar, protestors in Kalaburagi back Home Minister Priyank Kharge’s push for transparency

title: SIT moves Supreme Court to question officer in Gade Sai Krishna custodial death case
content: Suspended CI booked under SC and ST Act in the case of alleged abetment to suicide of Perupogu Kranthi Kumar

title: Bombay High Court commutes death sentence of two men in kidnap-murder case
content: The Bombay High Court has commuted the death sentence of two men to 30 years of rigorous imprisonment without remission for the kidnapping and murder of a young engineering student in Nashik in 2013.

title: NIA-led team arrests two in connection with killing of six Naga civilians in Manipur
content: Police said that a woman, Ayingbi, and another person, Pradip, were apprehended from the Kuki-Zo village

title: Exploring monsoon trails in the Eastern Ghats
content: As mist, rain and fresh greenery transform the Eastern Ghats, guided treks are drawing adventurers to summit trails, waterfalls and remote hill villages

title: Cyber Crime Police file case against Facebook user over fake poster targeting Congress leader V.M. Sudheeran
content: According to police, the fabricated poster falsely attributed a statement to Sudheeran as criticising and insulting Kerala CM V.D. Satheesan, and carried both their photos

title: Endowments Commissioner faults slow pace of Kanaka Durga temple works, orders shift work
content: Endowments Commissioner tells temple authorities to ensure completion of new laddu preparation facility and Annadanam building before the commencement of the month of Sravana

title: Bapatla Collector seeks help of political parties for smooth conduct of SIR
content: Voters who fail to submit SIR forms will have to apply for fresh enrolment through Form-6 with the required supporting documents, says Collector

title: Record 1.3 lakh pilgrims visit Amarnath shrine in 5 days in Kashmir
content: Our goal is to make the pilgrimage a truly memorable experience for everyone and they have access to the best possible amenities, says Lieutenant Governor Manoj Sinha

title: Madras High Court clears the decks for release of Idhayam Murali
content: Justice K. Kumaresh Babu refuses to restrain the release of the movie at the instance of film director Sudha Kongara

title: Nanotechnology conference Bengaluru INDIA NANO 2026 in August; SVU announces event
content: Sri Venkateswara University and the Centre for Nano and Soft Matter Sciences announced the August 3-5 conference in Tirupati, aiming to interest degree students in nanoscience

title: China's mega dam on Brahmaputra facing major natural threat: Report
content: The warning comes at a time when Beijing's massive dam project has already become a geopolitical flashpoint. India has repeatedly expressed concerns over China's upstream activities on the Brahmaputra, citing potential risks to water security, ecology and downstream communities. The latest study now raises fresh questions over whether the project also carries significant geological risks acknowledged by Chinese scientists themselves.

title: Actor Rajpal Yadav gets 3 months jail as HC upholds conviction in cheque bounce cases
content: The Delhi High Court upheld actor Rajpal Yadav's conviction in multiple cheque bounce cases.  The court sentenced the actor to three months imprisonment for these offenses.  This judgment dismissed petitions challenging the trial court's earlier decision.  Yadav had previously paid approximately Rs 4.25 crore to the complainant company.  He is also facing a separate loan dispute case with a large outstanding amount.

title: 'We would pour extra water...': Ex-batting coach reveals Kohli's England preparation
content: Former batting coach Sanjay Bangar detailed Virat Kohli's intense preparation methods.  Kohli repeatedly practiced in Mumbai to simulate English swing and moisture.  This meticulous work helped Indian batsmen succeed in England previously.  The team now faces a challenging ODI series after a T20 disappointment.  Kohli and Rohit Sharma are expected to lead the batting attack.

title: OpenAI shuts down browser whose most-crucial 'Google detail' it didn’t tell users
content: OpenAI is discontinuing its Atlas web browser, which was launched less than a year ago. Many of Atlas's AI-powered browsing features are now moving into ChatGPT. A new Google Chrome extension will also offer these capabilities to users. The company is also upgrading its ChatGPT desktop app with a built-in browser. This strategic shift aims to consolidate AI browsing functionalities within existing platforms.

title: 'Bones beginning to show': Sonam Wangchuk loses 7.5 kg amid hunger strike
content: Sonam Wangchuk stated his hunger stabilized after thirteen days of fasting at Jantar Mantar. He asserted his constitutional right to peaceful protest should not be violated by authorities. Wangchuk supports students demanding accountability for examination irregularities and pending Ladakh issues. He believes the education minister's resignation could restore youth confidence politically. The protest continues with a planned march to Parliament on July 20.

title: Ram Mandir donation case: Supreme Court to hear pleas seeking CBI probe on July 13
content: The Supreme Court will hear petitions on July 13 concerning alleged Ram temple donation embezzlement. Three petitions seek a court-monitored CBI probe into financial irregularities. Petitioners also demand a forensic audit of the Trust's entire finances. An investigation is underway in Uttar Pradesh, with eight accused arrested. Temple trust members express faith in the ongoing investigation and administration.

title: Heat of Europe's AC ban comes to New York; Mayor Mamdani shares AC tips
content: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani urged residents to reduce electricity use. He asked people to adjust AC settings and limit unnecessary power consumption. The city is also implementing similar measures in government buildings. Officials aim to reduce pressure on the power grid during the heat wave. This appeal comes as electricity demand rises across New York City.

title: Tiny mouse survives near 7,000 metres on volcanoes, scientists ask how
content: The Andean leaf-eared mouse thrives at extreme altitudes, a feat previously thought impossible for mammals. This tiny rodent lives above 6,700 meters, enduring thin oxygen and brutal cold. Researchers found highland mice possess superior heat generation and efficient energy use for survival. Genomic studies reveal adaptations for processing plant chemicals and managing toxins. These findings offer potential insights for human medicine and understanding life's resilience.

title: Why that colourful cloth on hotel beds is more useful than you think
content: Hotel bed runners add color and texture, enhancing the room's visual appeal and brand identity. These fabrics provide a designated spot for luggage, protecting the main bedding from dirt. Housekeeping benefits as runners are easier and cheaper to clean or replace than entire duvet covers. However, some hotels are removing them due to hygiene concerns after the pandemic.

title: Rishabh Agrawal on Meta offer: It was far higher than $1 million
content: Rishabh Agrawal, an IIT Bombay graduate, declined a substantial offer from Meta. He chose to leave the company to establish his own artificial intelligence startup. Agrawal's decision reflects a growing trend among young engineers in India. Many are now prioritizing entrepreneurship over corporate roles in deep tech. This move highlights a shift in the nation's burgeoning tech ecosystem.

title: 'Cops could have alerted us ... I believed them': CM Vijay recalls Karur stampede
content: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay addressed a public meeting in Karur. He distributed compassionate appointment orders to 32 families affected by a stampede. The chief minister described the stampede as the most painful time of his life. He alleged that the Tamil Nadu Police had staged drama during the incident. This visit marked Vijay's first official trip to Karur since assuming office.

title: 'They offered namaz ...': CM Yogi slams SP, Cong for 'appeasement politics' amid Ayodhya row
content: Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath accused opposition parties of appeasement politics. He alleged they disrespected Hindu faith by allowing prayers at Hanumangarhi temple. Adityanath stated these parties opposed Ayodhya's development and Ram temple construction. He highlighted the current government's role in building the grand Ram temple. The chief minister also mentioned opposition to Ayodhya's transformation and new projects.

title: 'It's really going badly': India coach puts onus on players to change mindset
content: India's assistant coach Ryan ten Doeschate expressed disappointment with the team's overseas T20I results. The team has now lost consecutive bilateral T20I series against England and Ireland. Ten Doeschate emphasised the need for players to accept underachievement in foreign conditions. He urged the squad to focus on excelling in challenging overseas environments for future tournaments. India aims to avoid a whitewash in the final T20I against England.

title: Be ready for Op Sindoor 2.0, Op Snow Leopard 2.0: Army chief to officers
content: Army Chief General Dhiraj Seth stressed combat readiness and holistic preparedness for officers. He instructed them to validate operational readiness through spot reviews and maintain mission readiness. Officers must think practically and arrive at simple, implementable solutions for challenges. The army's role extends beyond borders, contributing to Viksit Bharat by 2047. General Seth urged officers to lead by example and uphold high ethical standards.

title: India set to see 25% rise in petroleum product exports
content: Although India imports around 90% of its crude oil requirement, it has become one of the world's largest exporters of refined petroleum products by using its large and sophisticated refineries.

title: Delhi schools to conduct 'good touch, bad touch' sessions under POCSO awareness drive
content: Delhi schools will conduct good touch and bad touch awareness sessions this month. Child protection committees will be established in all schools by July's end. Teachers and staff will receive training on the POCSO Act and child safety protocols. The campaign involves police, counsellors, and anganwadi workers supporting the initiative. Students will also receive self-defence training and gender sensitisation sessions.

title: ‘Search my name’: Haaland's cheeky post as Google joins viral 'Viking Row' craze
content: Norway's Erling Haaland encouraged fans to search his name on Google. This action revealed an animated Viking Row celebration, a popular tradition. Haaland also revived his forgotten rap career with a new song remix. He has scored seven goals in the FIFA World Cup tournament. Norway now faces England in the quarter-finals after a historic win.

title: Bengaluru class 8 girl dies by suicide; death note cites ‘humiliation’ at school
content: A fourteen-year-old student died by suicide after alleged humiliation at her school. She left a note expressing distress over being blamed for something she did not do. The student had previously complained to the headmaster about unfair treatment by teachers. Authorities are investigating the death note and a separate complaint from other students. An assistant cook has been removed from her job following allegations of abusive behavior.

title: E20 costlier to produce than pure petrol: Government
content: The petroleum ministry clarified E20 petrol production costs versus pure petrol. Ethanol blending protects Indian consumers from volatile international oil prices. This program has saved India significant foreign exchange and crude oil imports. It also provides greater fuel price stability and increases farmers' incomes. The transition to E20 has been a phased process with stakeholder consultations.

title: India should not blink first in US trade negotiations, says SBI report
content: India should avoid early trade concessions with the United States. Washington is using uncertainty as a bargaining instrument across many issues. India possesses unique strategic strengths that should be leveraged. The nation should play the long game and test US resolve. This approach will improve India's bargaining position over time.

title: 'Dictator' Mbappe dispatches Morocco with disdain
content: On Day 29 of the World Cup, France handed Morocco a harsh footballing lesson as Mbappe dismissed the Moroccan side with disdain. Also read the preview for Belgium vs Spain in today's Offside.

title: 'Dumbfounded. Gobsmacked': Tharoor's scathing reaction to India's England humiliation
content: England secured an emphatic three-nil series victory over India in the T20I matches. Congress MP Shashi Tharoor expressed his profound disappointment and speechlessness following the defeat. Captain Shreyas Iyer acknowledged the team's shortcomings in all aspects of the game. Assistant coach Ryan ten Doeschate admitted India's underperformance in foreign conditions.

title: A weak monsoon could trigger a storm in India's economy
content: A weaker monsoon poses risks to India's rural economy and farm incomes. This could increase food inflation and slow down consumption demand in coming months. Agriculture and related sectors face the biggest impact from below-normal rainfall. Microfinance institutions are more vulnerable than banks due to rural borrower exposure. Hydroelectric generation may decline, but broader economic outlook remains supported.

title: 'She asked for help': CCTV footage shows 'bullying' moments before Jaipur schoolgirl's death
content: Fresh CCTV footage has been released by a family alleging bullying caused their daughter's death. The footage reportedly shows repeated bullying inside the classroom shortly before the incident. The family seeks action against those responsible under the Juvenile Justice Act. They also question the incomplete police chargesheet and omitted school officials. The case highlights accountability issues and calls for mandatory anti-bullying protocols in schools.

title: 'Definitely handball': Morocco coach questions Mbappé goal as VAR no-show sparks debate
content: Morocco's coach questioned a handball in Kylian Mbappé's goal during their World Cup quarter-final loss. He admitted France deserved their victory after a difficult first half for his team. Morocco struggled with possession transitions and lacked creativity against the strong French side. Despite the defeat, the coach believes his team is closing the gap on elite nations.

title: IAF ranks 3rd in global airpower, outflanks China for 5th time since 2022; key gaps remain
content: The Indian Air Force secured third place globally in the 2026 WDMMA rankings. This ranking places India's air power ahead of China's People's Liberation Army Air Force. The World Directory of Modern Military Aircraft uses a proprietary True Value Rating system. This system evaluates air forces based on fleet size and composition. India's air force is undergoing modernization and procurement programs to enhance capabilities.

title: 48-hour miracle: How Delhi just did something it hasn't done in 3 years
content: Delhi's air quality reached the 'good' category for the first time in nearly three years. Continuous monsoon rains washed pollutants away, lowering the Air Quality Index significantly. This event highlights how quickly weather can cleanse the city's atmosphere. However, maintaining this improvement remains a significant environmental challenge for Delhi. Experts emphasize that year-round emission reduction policies are crucial for sustained clean air.

title: A missile launch, a nuclear message: Why China wanted the US to take notice
content: China recently launched a long-range missile from a submarine into the South Pacific. This action demonstrated Beijing's growing nuclear power and strategic reach. The test aimed to send a message primarily to the United States. Pacific nations expressed concern over the provocative and destabilizing act. This event highlights China's ongoing military modernization efforts.

title: Watch: Dejected Sooryavanshi walks off even before catch; reaction goes viral
content: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi faced disappointment after his dismissal in the fourth T20I match.  He scored low scores in his first three international matches.  Jofra Archer troubled the young batter with short-pitched bowling.  Sooryavanshi came with high expectations after a sensational IPL and U-19 World Cup.  India trails England three-nil in the ongoing five-match series.

title: Gold price prediction: Will gold price see a bullish movement? Check today's outlook
content: ​​Gold price prediction today: Gold prices - MCX Gold August futures - are seeing a positive bias and a buy on dips strategy is favoured, says Jateen Trivedi, VP Research Analyst - Commodity and Currency, LKP Securities.

title: Madras High Court refuses to halt 'Idhayam Murali' release in Sudha Kongara plea
content: The Madras High Court refused to halt the release of 'Idhayam Murali' film. Filmmaker Sudha Kongara sought unpaid dues from the makers of 'Parasakthi'. Dawn Pictures stated that Kongara received substantial payments for her work. The production house cited financial losses and legal battles during 'Parasakthi's' making. The court permitted the film's release while the dispute continues.

title: New EPF Scheme 2026: 10 questions employees are asking about contributions, withdrawals & more
content: EPF Scheme 2026: The good news is that many core features of the provident fund system remain familiar. Existing PF accounts continue, contribution rates broadly remain unchanged and accumulated balances remain protected.

title: Watch: Morocco fans clash with police in London; Paris stays calm after France win
content: Morocco's World Cup journey concluded with a loss to France, leading to significant fan disturbances in London.  Riots erupted on Edgware Road, with police confronting crowds throwing projectiles.  Paris experienced largely peaceful celebrations despite initial concerns about potential unrest.  Earlier, Moroccan supporters clashed with police in The Hague after a Round-of-16 victory.

title: Rare bird spotted in Corbett Tiger Reserve; Conservationists hail it as positive sign
content: A rare Greater Painted-Snipe with chicks was documented in Corbett Tiger Reserve. This sighting provides the first scientific evidence of successful breeding for the species. The observation indicates a healthy wetland ecosystem within the protected reserve. Such breeding records are valuable for understanding species distribution and conservation effectiveness. This discovery highlights the importance of protecting natural habitats for vulnerable bird species.

title: BJP councillor, 2 aides booked for assaulting Mahad council chief
content: An FIR was filed against a BJP councillor and two aides for assaulting an administrative officer. The assault occurred after the councillor questioned the non-resolution of a damaged water pipeline. The chief officer was reportedly hit with an umbrella and punched during the altercation. Council employees struck work demanding the immediate arrest of the accused individuals. Police have issued notices for cooperation in the ongoing investigation and court proceedings.

title: Principal calls student a thief, shows CCTV footage in class; court refuses to drop FIR
content: The Himachal Pradesh High Court refused to quash an FIR against a school principal. She was accused of calling a student a thief in front of classmates. A psychologist's report finding no trauma did not halt criminal proceedings. The court stated mental trauma needs proof during the trial stage. Allegations, if true, clearly amount to a crime, the court held.

title: 15 Killed In Philippines Landslide As Typhoon Bavi Nears Taiwan
content: Two landslides triggered by heavy rains driven by Bavi killed at least 15 people and left six others missing on the Philippines' southern island of Mindanao, authorities said.

title: 23% Karnataka Youth Neither Working, Nor Studying. Bengaluru Bears The Load
content: Despite Karnataka's free bus travel scheme for women, women in 20 of the state's 31 districts still face restrictions on travelling alone to markets.

title: France Returns 23 Syrian Treasures After 15 Years As Macron Visits Damascus
content: The artifacts include Roman bronze objects, Byzantine and Islamic-era pieces and a richly coloured mosaic panel that once adorned the Umayyad Mosque.

title: Adani Partners With French Firm To Develop Low-Carbon Chemicals In India
content: The partnership marks Adani Group's entry into low-carbon chemicals, expanding beyond its renewable energy and infrastructure businesses.

title: Why US Once Exploded A Large Nuclear Bomb In Space
content: The US carried out the Starfish Prime test by detonating a 1.4-megaton thermonuclear bomb about 400 kilometres above Earth.

title: Chits In Walls, Meets In Park: How Italian Spies Sold NATO Secrets To Rus
content: The 59-year-old retired spy received Euro 4,000 in cash, the agreed-upon price for each highly classified piece of information, after every exchange.

title: Nigel Farage's Election Gamble Backfires. He Will Now Fight A 'Garbage Can'
content: Nigel Farage resigned as the member of parliament for Clacton-on-Sea in southeast England on Tuesday amid growing controversy over alleged undeclared financial donations.

title: Can You Lose Pension Over Absence From Work? Orissa High Court Draws The Line
content: The legal dispute dates back to disciplinary proceedings initiated against a railways employee after he remained away from duty.

title: Sheikh Hasina To Return To Bangladesh By December, Surrender With Colleagues
content: Sheikh Hasina fled Bangladesh in 2024 after protests ended her 20 years as prime minister across multiple terms.

title: China Successfully Tests Rocket Recovery System, Catches Up With Falcon9
content: The Long March-10B carrier rocket, a reusable liquid-fueled commercial launch vehicle, stands approximately 63 meters tall with a diameter of five meters.

title: Deepak Nandal: A Wanted Gangster Whose Shooters Died In Gurugram Encounter
content: The Haryana Special Task Force (STF) has taken action against several members of the Deepak Nandal gang in recent months.

title: Anti-Terror Agency-Led Team Arrests 2 Over Killing Of 6 Naga Civilians In Manipur
content: The development comes a day after the Naga Students' Federation (NSF) sought the intervention of President Droupadi Murmu, demanding justice for the victims and stronger measures to restore peace and security in the conflict-hit state

title: Political Row Over Kaleshwaram Intensifies After KTR's Kannepally Visit
content: Political tensions rise as Congress and BRS dispute Kaleshwaram irrigation project management and costs.

title: Elon Musk Buries Beef With Frenemy, Calls Rival Obvious AI Leader
content: Elon Musk seems to be burying his hatchet with Anthropic after bashing them repeatedly through the past year.

title: Drunk Man Steals Bus In Telangana, Drives 21 Km Before Crashing Into Toll Gate
content: The bus driver had completed his scheduled trip on Wednesday night and parked the vehicle at the bus stand before leaving for home.

title: 4 Gangsters Killed In Gurugram, A Look At Major Shootouts In Delhi-NCR
content: Gurugram witnessed one of the most high-profile and sensational encounters, in which the police killed four shooters of fugitive gangster Deepak Nandal.

title: Stock Market LIVE Updates, Sensex Today: Sensex Jumps 750 Points Higher, Asian Markets Advance
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty LIVE Updates: Brent crude traded near $76 while US West Texas Intermediate was below $72 per barrel.

title: K Kavitha Alleges Inaction Over Salkam Cheruvu Lake Encroachment Claims
content: TRS chief Kavitha alleged delay in action by officials and questioned the approach of HYDRAA in dealing with alleged encroachments on water bodies.

title: Over 40 Students Hospitalised After Eating Khichdi At Telangana Hostel
content: Over 40 students fell ill with suspected food poisoning after breakfast at a Telangana tribal school.

title: Bill Gates Heir's Shopping App Took Credit For Sales It Didn't Drive
content: Phia registered fake clicks on retailers' websites, allowing it to replace another referrer's unique code with its own, according to separate testing by Edelman, Capital One Shopping and Bloomberg.

title: India notifies rules for UK FTA vehicle import benefits ahead of July 15 rollout
content: The TOI News Desk comprises a dedicated and tireless team of journalists who operate around the clock to deliver the most current and comprehensive news and updates to the readers of The Times of India worldwide.
